prerequisites: A TickTick account (sign up at ticktick.com) to access the TickTick Developer portal.
A registered application in the TickTick Developer portal, which issues the Client ID and Client Secret used for OAuth2. See the official docs at https://developer.ticktick.com/api.
A redirect URI that you register with your application and that matches the value used during the OAuth2 authorization request.
Completion of the OAuth2 authorization flow to obtain a token, which is supplied through the connector's authorization config.

authMethods.name: OAuth2 (Authorization Code)
authMethods.summary: The TickTick Open API uses OAuth2. Register an application to obtain a Client ID (authorization.client_id) and Client Secret (authorization.client_secret), then run the authorization-code flow to obtain a token. The connector stores the token in authorization.bearer_token (or authorization.client_access_token) and sends it as a Bearer token on API requests.

setupSteps.title: Create a TickTick account
setupSteps.body: Sign up for or sign in to a TickTick account, which is required to register a developer application and authorize API access. Reference: TickTick Open API (https://developer.ticktick.com/api).

setupSteps.title: Register an application to get Client ID and Client Secret
setupSteps.body: In the TickTick Developer portal, register an application to obtain the Client ID and Client Secret, and set the redirect URI for your app. These map to the connector's authorization.client_id and authorization.client_secret secret fields. Reference: https://developer.ticktick.com/api.

setupSteps.title: Authorize and obtain a token
setupSteps.body: Complete the OAuth2 authorization-code flow using your Client ID, Client Secret, and registered redirect URI to exchange an authorization code for a token. The resulting token populates the connector's authorization.bearer_token (or authorization.client_access_token), which is sent as a Bearer token on requests.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector's authorization
setupSteps.body: In the source-ticktick connector, fill in the authorization config object with your OAuth2 credentials (client_id, client_secret) and the obtained token. The authorization field is the connector's single configurable input that carries these credentials.
